title: I went to my friend's factory for the first time.
description: On Friday evening, July 7, a Friend A, who is a developer at a large entertainment agency, contacted me to have lunch the next day. I really want t...

children.value:
, who is a developer at a large entertainment agency, contacted me to have lunch the next day.
I really want to go out when I'm at home, but I was wondering what to do because I got lazy when I had to go out.
As soon as I thought about it, I immediately answered sushi to his message about what I wanted to eat.

The next day at lunch, a friend drove his car to pick me up 30 minutes earlier than scheduled.
When I asked if he bought it because it was a car he hadn't seen before, he said he inherited the car that his father-in-law was riding.
Married friends always told me to enjoy my solo life, but it was a moment when I suddenly wanted to get married.

props.alt: Sushi that my Friend A bought me

children.value: I talked about all sorts of things while eating sushi that my

bought me.
Then naturally, the topic of conversation shifted about how other friends were doing, and then we talked about
who runs a factory in the suburbs outside of Seoul.
because he drove out anyway, and I contacted

right away.
He said it would be difficult to see him for a long time because it was his father's birthday, but he thought about whether to go or not because he said it would be good if we came to meet.
Still, I thought I wouldn't be able to see you for a long time unless it was this time, so I said I would go back after seeing you briefly, and I left for my friend's factory.

props.alt: A factory run by Friend B

children.value: We arrived at the factory after an hour's drive, and
's father greeted us on behalf of

, who went to the client for a while.
We went empty-handed because we didn't know we would see him, but I thought it was polite to give him a gift, so I hurriedly went out to the city to buy a cake and give it to him as a gift, and he thanked me and invited me to dinner.

who returned from the client and talked about the recent situation with each other, and especially talked a lot about how the factory is operated.
It was just amazing that my friend, who played with me immaturely as a child, became the president and ordered his subordinates to work. Also, I could feel that he had truly become the president, from his expression of skillfully handling the machine and seriously explaining the technology.
It was fun to talk to
, but We put off the unfinished story and followed him to the dinner table because we shouldn't delay the dinner.

props.alt: in the middle of setting up dinner

children.value: The dinner was attended by the parents of

, his sister's couple, and two nephews.
I sat down and exchanged glances with
and regretted, 'I should have just refused and had a quick cup of coffee with

The meal time has been quite long because you made so much food that there is not enough space to put even after attaching two tables.
Therefore, we talked a lot for a long time, and contrary to our concerns, we had a very fun time and even told us to sleep a day, so I sincerely agonized.
However, we returned to Seoul before it was too late because
promised to pick up his wife.
On my way back. As it started to get dark and cool, I felt emotional, so I came back talking about the old days with my

.
It was fun and nice to think of when I was young, but on the other hand, I felt strange when I realized that I was getting older.

title: Create Mocks with Mockito in Unit Test
description: Mockito is a Mocking framework that is used to create Mock objects to control situations that developers cannot control. However, if it is possible...

children.value: 💡 If you are not familiar with the terminology for test double, read
Let's find out about Spies, Stubs, Fakes, and Mocks

Mockito is a Mocking framework that is used to create Mock objects to control situations that developers cannot control.
However, if it is possible not to use the Mock object through efforts such as changing the code design, doing so can help write good code.

Mockito.mock() allows you to create a Mock object, and returns the default value for each type if you do not make any settings.
The default values are summarized as follows.
if the return value is void, nothing happens.
If the return value is of the Primitive type, it returns the default value of the primitive type.
if the return value is of the Object type, Returns
If the return value is of the Optional type, Returns
If the return value is of the Collection type, Returns empty collection.
If the way of using the annotation is convenient, it can be used.
To share between tests, the Mocks may be declared in the field to minimize redundancy.

Spies can also be created in a similar way to Mocks.
As mentioned in the article related to the test double, it acts like a real object if there is no setting.
If you use the annotation, you can only declare on the field.
Let's find out how to stub so that the Mocks can return the specified value.
Stubbing can be done by combining
💡 Since the example used in the Mocks and Stub creation chapter is used as it is, the overlapping parts will be excluded.
: Literal values can be specified according to the return type of method that enters when clause.
: Running a method that meets the conditions returns an exception.
: When a method meets the conditions, it executes a callback.
: Running a method that meets the conditions returns the execution value of the actual method.
The method of stubbing is the same as when arguments are not included, but there is a difference in that only the specified literal value argument or argument type is stubbed.
Only the corresponding literal value argument is stubbed, and using other arguments returns the default value as mentioned above.

You can specify the type to hand over to the argument.
Each matchers names are intuitive, so no explanation is required, so only examples of use are attached.
When Matchers and literal values are applied together, Matchers have priority.
is a setting that applies a loose rule, and does not cause an error even if a specified stub is not used.

In addition to the above-mentioned matchers, there are various matchers.
If you want to know about this, please refer to

Only stubbing that causes an exception is possible.
This is because the return value can be stubbed, but a compilation error occurs because the method return type is void.
Throws must be declared in the method specified to cause a Checked Exception.
Let's find out how to stub when calling the same method with the same argument.
If the same operation is performed and the literal value is different, stubbing can be performed using a varargs.
When stubbing multiple actions, perform a method chain.
calls, multiple stubbing with the same matchers or arguments is used, then each stubbing will override the previous one.
, have methods that start with do, such as

.
After that, we will call them

passes the Object type as an argument, the type safety is poor because it checks the type at runtime.
For this reason, it is recommended to use
When stubbing the void return type

when I stubbed the void in the Just the previous chapter.
Methods whose return type is void use
It is used to prevent side effects as the actual function is called when stubbing.
To verify that the specified method has been executed,
: Check how many times the specified method has been invoked. If the argument is not passed, use the default values of
: Check the method is invoked at least/most once.
: Check that at least/most the specified number of invokes has been made.
: Verify that the specified method is not invoked.
: Check that the specified Mock object has not interacted with anything.
: Check that methods are invoked in order.
💡 Even if the same method is called, it must be checked in order.
If you need more information about Verification mode, please refer to
Annotation that makes you inject both Mock and Spy objects.

title: Let's find out about Spies, Stubs, Fakes, and Mocks.
description: While searching for materials to learn about Mockito, I found a blogger calls fake object to a stub in an article about Test Double I thought all t...

children.value:
While searching for materials to learn about Mockito, I found a blogger calls fake object to a stub in an article about Test Double
I thought all the fake objects were Mocks, but I was wondering why they used the word Stub, so I looked into them.
Test double is derived from the stunt double that replaces the actor in the movie, and refers to
an object that proceeds on behalf of the object when it is difficult to proceed with the test.
In other words, it refers to an object that simplifies the components that SUT relies on so that it can focus only on the test target when testing.
Here, the test target is called SUT by shortening the System Under Test, and the component on which SUT depends is called DOC by shortening the Dependent-On Component.

props.alt: Isolation under test in unit test

children.value:
If the collaboration between SUT and DOC is not awkward, the function (behavior) is viewed as a unit and tested together is called a sociable test.
In the case of Sociable test, it is difficult to test situations that SUT cannot control, such as the current time, or code expensive to test such as Email.

Conversely, breaking the dependence of SUT and DOC and testing them on a single module basis is called a solitary test.
On the contrary, it is called solitary test to break the dependency of SUT and DOC, and test it in a single module unit.
The DOC created at this time is the test band.

💡 To describing the need for the Test Double, the sociable test was expressed somewhat negatively, but in reality, it is not.
Later, I will summarize the pros and cons of the Solitary test and Sociable test.
When you control an uncontrollable situation.

Random numbers, current time, and reading certain records from DB do not always guarantee the same results. In addition, network errors or external service conditions may not always be normal. In this way, it can be used to control situations that SUT cannot control as consistent situations.

Templates sent by e-mail, SMS, or messenger, or message contents sent to message queues are difficult to test because of the high cost of testing. In this case, the contents necessary for verification may be stored in the Test Double and then verified.
When you build an environment for creating tests.
It can be used if a specific function is not yet implemented and testing is not possible.
It is the most basic test double, which
is used only to pass over to the parameter value and is not actually used

.
This includes an object with only a default value filled in the field or an object with an empty method implementation unit.
A step up from dummies, stubs are typically

The same operation as the actual object may be performed or may be operated as a stub.
When called,
it stores the data needed for test verification
an object implemented by simplifying the operation of an actual object

, and although it is not hard-coded and operates dynamically, it is not suitable for use in a production environment.
An example is an in-memory DB using HashMap.
an object created dynamically through the Mock library

.
Depending on the configuration, it works like Dummy, Stub, or Spy.
💡 When a Spy object is created using a Mock library or testing framework,
it usually behaves like a real object and can optionally be used by stubbing the desired method.
I've checked on Mockito, Mockk, Spock, and Jest that I'm interested in, but if there's anything else, please let me know.
A mock behave like a dummy
A mock behave like a stub
A mock behave like a spy
It has low resistance to refactoring.

Low resistance to refactoring is in line with increasing code management costs.
Let's look at the example.
When using the Test double, the internal implementation of the SUT is externally exposed as follows.
At this time, if the method name or parameter of the DOC changes, in other words,
if the method signature changes, the test code must also be modified.

In the case of the Test double created manually, Since stubbing is performed outside the test, redundancy is reduced, so refactoring resistance is little less.
However, if you use the Mock library, you suffer from having to modify all the tests because you stub inside the test.

When you write stubbing, you write it expecting the DOC to actually work in production like that.
However, the real object may not work as expected.
The amount of code can be too much.
The amount of code can be too much for the reason mentioned at the end in

Although each test double was summarized as simply as possible to make it easy to distinguish, it may not be clearly distinguished when working on the actual project.
In fact, this is not unusual, and MSDN Magazine uses the following figure as an example and states that, unlike theory, the difference between each test double is actually ambiguous.
I think I can't do anything else if I focus on terms, so I'm going to focus on the role of the object.
Exploring The Continuum Of Test Doubles
What's the difference between a mock & stub?
Stub: Mocking Object For Tests In Yuk Recycle
Mock object abuse ruins the test code. - Korean

title: Starting unit test with JUnit 5 Part 1
description: I've been aware of the importance of testing code for a long time, but I've been delaying them, saying that I'll learn later because I can finish m...

children.value:
I've been aware of the importance of testing code for a long time, but I've been delaying them, saying that I'll learn later because I can finish my work faster if I write a code during the time I study for the testing code.
However, whenever the requirements were added little by little, I suddenly felt ashamed of myself for testing them in a fist style.
Therefore, I am going to write a series to
In this article, I will summarize the information that must be known, but can proceed with the test properly.

A unit test is a procedure in computer programming to verify that certain modules of source code work exactly as intended.
That is, it refers to the procedure of writing a test case for all functions and methods.
Detect bug: The functionality is tested in small pieces, so you can quickly check where the problem occurred.
Refactoring: ensures better accuracy and stability in refectoring because it makes the code small and simple.
Documentation: Because there are libraries that use unit tests to document APIs, documentation and maintenance become convenient.
Ensuring results: because the method is isolated and simulated, the results of the unit test do not always represent the results of the system as a whole.
Development and Maintenance Costs: Test codes also need to be developed and maintained, increasing the scope to be managed.
Unlike the previous JUnit, JUnit 5 consists of JUnit Platform + JUnit Jupiter + JUnit Vintage.
: It is responsible for executing the test framework on the JVM. It also provides a test engine API to facilitate integration with JUnit.
: It provides a test engine for Jupiter subprojects such as JUnit 5.
: It provides a test engine for running JUnit 3 and JUnit 4 based tests.
Since it is frequently used, I am going to organize the annotations that I basically need to know.
: Indicates that the method is a
: Indicates that this is a method that must be
: Indicates that this is a method that must be
are called life cycle methods because they run according to the life cycle of the test.

first, and even if I think about it in alphabetical order, it would be run first, but it did later.

This is because JUnit runs the tests in an unpredictable order, as unit tests should not normally depend on order.
However, sometimes the order of testing may be important.
In this case, annotate your test class or test interface with
Sort the methods in ascending order based on the value specified in the

annotation.
The default order value is
, and a method larger than this value runs later than a method without

, and each element performs the following steps from the beginning.
Check that the values of each element are the same. - If the same, move on to the next element.
If not, check the regular expression with

children.value:
The assertion method does not execute subsequent code because an exception occurs when the test fails.
However, there may be cases where you want to run all assertion methods regardless of failure.
In that case, if you use this method to group the assertion methods, they do not cause an exception, so even
if they fail, they do not stop immediately and aggregate the errors
But, if an exception (e.g., Out of Memory) is generated in the blacklist, the execution is immediately stopped and overwritten with an unchecked exception.

children.value:
JUnit creates a new test instance for each test method to minimize the impact between tests.
However, you may not want to create a new test instance every time as needed.
In this case, you can annotate the class

to change the test instance creation pattern according to the mode you specify.
The selectable modes are

:
It is the default value and is characterized by
because it creates a new instance for each method.

:
It is possible to share the status in the method because each class creates a new instance.
do not necessarily have to be declared in the
Why do we need to understand the life cycle of a test instance
If we don't know the life cycle of the test instance, we write the code on the premise that we can share the state because we think in

mode.
Therefore, the following cases may occur.

would start from zero every time.

title: Extracting a list of time zones with Java +Kotlin
description: I searched online because the service I was developing needed timezone and offset information, but I couldn't find the format I wanted. Therefore...

children.value:
I searched online because the service I was developing needed timezone and offset information, but I couldn't find the format I wanted.
Therefore, processing was necessary, but I wanted to process it into a code so that it could be easily managed in the future.
I remember seeing the API that brings up the list of zones on the time package side, so when I looked it up, I found
, and I succeeded in extracting the list using it, so I summarized it.

props.code:
Etc/GMT+12 (UTC-12:00)
Pacific/Pago_Pago (UTC-11:00)
Pacific/Samoa (UTC-11:00)
Pacific/Niue (UTC-11:00)
US/Samoa (UTC-11:00)
...

children.value: The reason for using replace for offset is that if it is +-0, it is written as

like other offsets.
Therefore, it was used to unify the form.
Without sorting, it is randomly mixed, not in name order or offset order. Therefore, I ordered it in ascending order based on offset.
It's not that different, but I've also written Kotlin code.
